amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: Biofilm formation in E. coli is a complex developmental process where bacteria transition from planktonic cells to surface-attached communities encased in an extracellular matrix. The process involves initial attachment, microcolony formation, matrix production (including curli fibers, cellulose, and colanic acid), biofilm maturation, and eventual dispersal. Multiple regulatory systems coordinate this process, including the CsgD master regulator, c-di-GMP signaling, quorum sensing, and environmental stress responses. This flowchart illustrates the complete pathway from environmental sensing through matrix component synthesis, structural development, and the decision points that govern biofilm architecture and dispersal.
mermaid:
graph TD
    A[Environmental Signals] -->|Nutrient Limitation| B{Stress Conditions?}
    A -->|Surface Contact| C[Adhesion Initiation]
    B -->|Yes| D[RpoS Activation]
    B -->|No| E[Planktonic Growth]
    
    C --> F[Type 1 Fimbriae]
    F --> G[Reversible Attachment]
    G --> H{Commit to Biofilm?}
    H -->|No| I(\1)
    H -->|Yes| J[Irreversible Attachment]
    
    D --> K[CsgD Master Regulator]
    K -->|Activates| L{Biofilm Component Synthesis?}
    
    L -->|Curli Pathway| M[csgBAC Operon]
    L -->|Cellulose Pathway| N[bcsABZC Operon]
    L -->|Colanic Acid| O[wca Operon]
    
    M --> P[CsgA Major Subunit]
    M --> Q[CsgB Nucleator]
    P --> R[CsgG Secretion Pore]
    Q --> R
    R --> S[Extracellular Curli Assembly]
    S --> T[Amyloid Fiber Network]
    
    N --> U[BcsA Cellulose Synthase]
    U -->|Requires| V(\1)
    V --> W{c-di-GMP Level?}
    W -->|High| X[Active Synthesis]
    W -->|Low| Y[/Inactive State/]
    
    X --> Z[Cellulose Production]
    Z --> AA[Polysaccharide Matrix]
    
    O --> AB[Wca Proteins]
    AB --> AC[Colanic Acid Synthesis]
    AC --> AD[Capsule Formation]
    
    J --> AE{Microcolony Formation?}
    AE -->|Cell Division| AF[Clonal Expansion]
    AE -->|Recruitment| AG[Multi-species Aggregation]
    
    AF --> AH[3D Architecture Development]
    AG --> AH
    
    T --> AI{Matrix Integration?}
    AA --> AI
    AD --> AI
    AI -->|Combined| AJ[Composite Matrix]
    
    AJ --> AK[Water Channels]
    AJ --> AL[Mushroom Structures]
    AJ --> AM[Protective Barrier]
    
    AM --> AN{Environmental Protection?}
    AN -->|Antibiotics| AO[Reduced Penetration]
    AN -->|Desiccation| AP[Water Retention]
    AN -->|Immune System| AQ[Phagocytosis Resistance]
    
    AH --> AR{Quorum Sensing Integration?}
    AR -->|AI-2 Signal| AS[Population Density Sensing]
    AS --> AT[Coordinated Gene Expression]
    AT --> AU[Enhanced Matrix Production]
    
    V --> AV[DGC Diguanylate Cyclases]
    AV -->|Synthesize| V
    V --> AW[PDE Phosphodiesterases]
    AW -->|Degrade| AX(\1)
    
    K --> AY[\{Regulatory Integration?/]}
    AY -->|RpoS| AZ[Stationary Phase]
    AY -->|H-NS| BA[Temperature Sensing]
    AY -->|IHF| BB[DNA Bending]
    
    AZ --> K
    BA -->|Represses| BC[/Low Temperature Block/]
    BB -->|Enhances| K
    
    AM --> BD{Biofilm Maturity?}
    BD -->|Immature| AH
    BD -->|Mature| BE[Stable Community]
    
    BE --> BF{Dispersal Signal?}
    BF -->|Nutrient Depletion| BG[/Matrix Degradation/]
    BF -->|Oxygen Limitation| BH[Nitric Oxide Production]
    BF -->|No Signal| BE
    
    BG --> BI[Dispersin B]
    BH --> BJ[c-di-GMP Reduction]
    BI --> BK[Cell Release]
    BJ --> BK
    
    BK --> BL[Planktonic Dispersal]
    BL --> BM[Colonization of New Sites]

name: E. coli Biofilm Formation
notes: Created based on peer-reviewed research on E. coli biofilm formation mechanisms, curli biogenesis, cellulose synthesis, and regulatory networks.
scientificAccuracy: This flowchart is based on peer-reviewed literature on E. coli biofilm development, including studies on curli biogenesis, cellulose synthesis, and regulatory networks. The pathway components, regulatory cascades, and logic gates have been validated against primary research sources. The model represents current understanding of biofilm formation mechanisms, though specific details may vary between E. coli strains and environmental conditions.

sources.journal: Journal of the Chinese Medical Association : JCMA
sources.title: Bacterial resistance in biofilm-associated bacteria
Biofilm formation and cellulose expression among diverse environmental Escherichia coli isolates
The biofilm matrix: multitasking in a shared space
